media.base_medium: Modified M9 minimal medium (Sambrook & Russell M9 base [ref 2], modified as in Nerke et al. Metab Eng Commun 2024 [ref 3]), with the US(Fe)-trace element solution of Bühler et al. Biotechnol Bioeng 2003 [ref 4]. Growth-reference version = full modified-M9 recipe minus the biotransformation additives L-lysine and extra FeSO4.

added: confirmed medium name 'modified M9 medium with d-xylose', 20 g/L D-xylose, aerobic 30 C/200 rpm, pointer to Supplementary Table S1
full modified-M9 salts (Na2HPO4.H2O, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4)
US(Fe)-trace element solution fully expanded: FeSO4, CaCl2, MnCl2, ZnSO4, H3BO3, Na2MoO4, CuCl2, Na2EDTA, HCl

sources_chased.what: Base-medium references [2] Sambrook & Russell (M9), [3] Nerke et al. Metab Eng Commun 2024;18:e00241, [4] Bühler et al. Biotechnol Bioeng 2003;81:683-94 (US-Fe trace)
sources_chased.how: reference list in Supplementary Table S1; full composition already spelled out in S1 so no further fetch needed

media.notes: Complete recipe recovered verbatim from the paper's own Supplementary Table S1; the trace-element stock (US(Fe)) is fully expanded, no unexpanded named solution remains. The medium contains NO cobalt and NO nickel — the previous record's 'Co2+' component and 'Cobalt Co2+'/'Nickel Ni2+' uncovered entries are spurious (the US(Fe) trace solution has neither). Real trace-element gaps in the prior record were borate and molybdate. Iron is supplied only as Fe(II) (FeSO4); no ferric salt is in the base medium. This growth-reference version excludes L-lysine and the extra FeSO4 that were added only for biotransformation. Cultivation is aerobic (30 C, 200 rpm shake flasks; aerated stirred-tank bioreactor).
